calendar.matters:
Petition And Verified Application Assuming Jurisdiction Over Law Practice Of Susan Marie Griffin; Request For Interim Orders; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Therof; Declarations Of Gail Mullikin And Kara Hallsten
Off Calendar Mar-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER SUSAN GRIFFIN'S unopposed petition to assume jurisdiction over the law practice of Susan Marie Griffin is granted.

Petitioner filed and served the application in a timely fashion and its decision to employ a noticed motion rather than the OSC process per Business & Professions Code sec. 6180.4 is a non-issue. (See Eddy v. Temkin (1985) 167 Cal.App.3d 1115, 1120 ["An order to show cause is considered the equivalent of a notice of motion, but with a citation to appear and show cause annexed."]; Difani v. Riverside Cty. Oil Co. (1927) 201 Cal. 210, 213-14 ["An order to show cause is a notice of motion and a citation to the party to appear at a stated time and place to show cause why a motion should not be granted."]; Amjadi v. Brown (2021) 68 Cal.App.5th 383, 387 [rejecting distinction between OSC and motion and "turn[ing] to the merits of plaintiff's arguments."].)

Petitioner demonstrates good cause for the requested relief pursuant to Business & Professions Code sec. 6180.5. Petitioner's proposed relief is also consistent with that statutory provision.

Petitioner shall prepare a proposed order granting the motion that sets forth the requested relief and e-mail it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

Order is signed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)
